mentions.headline: Hays CISD considers employee compensation, librarian pay for 2026-27
mentions.content_excerpt: Community Impact on Hays CISD $12M budget cuts including 7 library position cuts; lead librarian model across campuses.

mentions.headline: As AI enters book ban disputes, here's what it means for school districts
mentions.author: Naaz Modan
mentions.content_excerpt: Deep Dive feature. New Braunfels ISD removed/restricted ~1,500 books, reviewed 3,000+ with AI. Steve quoted extensively: 'We want to be Switzerland,' 'If we win Texas, we win the country,' 'Librarians are aware of who are the loud voices out there... We're just showing them the data.' Moak Casey described as having equity in Bookmarked and backing SB 13 lawmakers. PEN America's Kasey Meehan and TASA's Kevin Brown quoted. $2-3/student pricing. Notes Bookmarked doesn't search book content, uses external data.
mentions.product_signal: NBISD using AI to review 3,000+ books. Internal logs obtained via public records request by Texas Freedom to Read Project.
key_quotes: We want to be Switzerland. It's so politically charged — we're just trying to be able to protect books, protect literature, and allow people to read what they want to read.
If we win Texas, we win the country. If we can solve it in Texas, we can solve it in the other states.
Librarians are aware of who are the loud voices out there... We're just showing them the data.
We went down that path of talking to school districts and figuring out: What is their problem, and how do we solve that?

mentions.headline: The Right Is Using AI Content Scanners to Try to Supercharge Book Banning
mentions.author: Claire Woodcock
mentions.content_excerpt: Frames Bookmarked alongside BLOCKADE, Rated Books, NarraTrue as tools supercharging book banning. Names NBISD ($9K contract). Librarian: 'I feel like BookmarkED is flagging more each time you run it.'
mentions.product_signal: NBISD librarian reports over-flagging. Route to /feedback:product.

mentions.headline: White Oak ISD book challenges have resulted in seven library removals this year
mentions.content_excerpt: East Texas local paper. 7 books removed from White Oak ISD library since January 2026 via challenge process. Includes Heartstopper Vol. 1 (LGBTQ graphic novel, Jan 6 meeting), The Perks of Being a Wallflower (moved to public library, March 2 vote 4-1).

mentions.headline: Records show New Braunfels ISD pulling, restricting more books than it has publicly listed
mentions.content_excerpt: Local SA affiliate investigation. Public records show NBISD removals exceed the 450 titles the district publicly lists; 600+ from high schools per Feb 2026 count, with 800+ from middle schools total. Books flagged by internal tools + third-party vendor resources.

mentions.headline: Librarian 'gobsmacked' after school uses AI to remove 200 books including 1984 and Twilight
mentions.author: Frankie Elliott
mentions.content_excerpt: International signal. UK school used AI to flag 200 books. Librarian refused, faced investigation, resigned. Shows AI-in-libraries narrative going global.

mentions.headline: The Rise of Book-Ban Technology
mentions.author: Elissa Malespina
mentions.content_excerpt: Critical analysis of AI book-screening tools including Bookmarked and Class-Shelf Plus. 40+ Texas districts, $138K contracts cited. Key concern: shifts framework from 'should this book be removed?' to 'why should it remain?' Notes AI tools can't legally access copyrighted book content. Predominantly cautionary/critical tone.

mentions.headline: U.S. Book Challenges Update: March 2026 Edition
mentions.content_excerpt: ALA March 2026 roundup of challenges. Names New Braunfels (1,500 titles pulled: Obama/Bush/Clinton memoirs, Shakespeare — March 2026). References broader Texas landscape; 4,235 titles challenged nationally in 2025.

mentions.headline: New Braunfels ISD Nears End of Book Reviews
mentions.content_excerpt: Right-leaning outlet covering NBISD approaching completion of SB 13 review. Positive framing toward district's enforcement action.

mentions.headline: The startup that turned Texas's book ban law into big business
mentions.author: Max Ufberg
mentions.content_excerpt: Profiles Bookmarked as profiting from Texas book-ban legislation. Headline frames it as 'big business.' Cites $80K+ lobbying spend for SB 13, Moak Casey hired. Steve quoted: 'We try to be Switzerland. And it's hard to be Switzerland.' 150 districts (mix of paid/free pilots). Angel funded, seeking additional capital. $3/student pricing upcoming. PEN America's Tasslyn Magnusson quoted critically. First half read; paywall blocked remainder.
key_quotes: We're just showing you what we find on the internet. We're not telling you what to do.
We try to be Switzerland. And it's hard to be Switzerland.

mentions.headline: New Braunfels ISD bans 600+ books, ages up 800+ titles using AI & overly-restrictive selection criteria
mentions.content_excerpt: Advocacy org records-based analysis of NBISD removals. Cites AI use and overly-restrictive selection. Documents 600+ removed HS books and 800+ aged-up middle school titles.

mentions.headline: This infamous school district banned 142 LGBTQ+ books just this year already
mentions.content_excerpt: Katy ISD pulled 142 LGBTQ+-themed books in the first month of 2026 per public records. Required by SB 12/SB 13 compliance.

mentions.headline: Cy-Fair ISD board votes to reinstate removed textbook chapters
mentions.content_excerpt: Newly-reconfigured CFISD board voted 4-3 to restore 13 previously-removed textbook chapters covering climate, vaccines, diversity, depopulation.

mentions.headline: New Braunfels ISD is gaslighting the community over its book removals
mentions.author: Frank Strong
mentions.content_excerpt: Analysis disputing NBISD's public framing of removal scope. Argues the public-facing list understates actual removals.

mentions.headline: Mapping One Semester of Texas School Book Bans
mentions.author: Frank Strong
mentions.content_excerpt: Interactive map and narrative cataloguing first half of 2025-26 school year in Texas. Names: New Braunfels (455 removed for review), Conroe (271 permanent removals), Hays CISD (~200), Lamar CISD (700+ incl. Pinkalicious), Leander (To Kill a Mockingbird, House on Mango Street via AI/SB 12), McAllen (Native Son), NEISD (Canterbury Tales, mythology, LGBTQ titles), Argyle (LGBTQ directive), Carroll ISD (Pretty Perfect Kitty Corn), La Grange (Butt or Face? book 3), San Marcos CISD (SLAC rejected challenges — positive), Abilene (thoughtful model — positive), Harlingen (Scholastic cancelled), Boerne (holiday book exchange cancelled).

mentions.headline: 2025 Year in Review
mentions.author: Sanhita SinhaRoy
mentions.content_excerpt: ALA flagship publication. Extensive coverage of book access issues. Named ZERO technology solutions. Bookmarked absent from the librarian conversation it needs to be in.

mentions.headline: New Braunfels ISD bans books written by Obama, Shakespeare and Matthew McConaughey
mentions.content_excerpt: Mentions Bookmarked in context of NBISD book removal decisions. References Texas Freedom to Read Project records requests.

mentions.headline: School libraries must comply with new laws on book content. This company is selling a solution.
mentions.content_excerpt: Syndicated Texas Standard piece. Public radio framing. 'Selling a solution' implies commercial opportunism.

mentions.headline: School libraries must comply with new laws on book content. This company is selling a solution.
mentions.content_excerpt: Original source of HPPR/KWBU/KTTZ syndication. $130K+ in contracts across Texas. Librarian feedback — some positive, some calling product a failure. NPR reach.
mentions.product_signal: Some librarians calling product a failure — route to /feedback:product.

mentions.headline: 'Antivirus for libraries': How a Texas startup is capitalizing on school book bans
mentions.author: Bayliss Wagner
mentions.content_excerpt: Investigative feature. Bookmarked as 'antivirus for libraries.' $130K+ statewide contracts. Killeen ISD ~$40K, Llano ISD ~$3K. Pricing 25¢-$2/student. New Braunfels flagged 450 books. Key finding: <0.04% parent usage across 8 districts studied. Librarian complaints: product 'did nothing that it said it was going to do effectively.' Syndicated to Texas Standard, HPPR, KTTZ, KWBU.
mentions.product_signal: Low parent adoption (<0.04%) and librarian complaint about effectiveness. Route to /feedback:product.

mentions.headline: Leander ISD pulls 11 books, graphic novels from libraries after review
mentions.content_excerpt: Local CBS affiliate coverage. Leander ISD pulls 11 books and graphic novels after compliance review under new state laws.

mentions.headline: Leander ISD removes books, graphic novels from classroom libraries
mentions.content_excerpt: Austin NBC affiliate. Companion to CBS Austin coverage of 11-book pull in Leander.

mentions.headline: McKinney ISD Finally Removes Sexually Explicit Books
mentions.content_excerpt: Right-leaning outlet. Framed positively — district completing review and removing targeted titles.

mentions.headline: Texas School Districts are Overwhelmed by New Library Laws: AI is Not the Answer
mentions.content_excerpt: Advocacy commentary arguing AI is not an appropriate tool for SB 13 compliance. References Leander AI use.

mentions.headline: School districts turn to AI to comply with new state laws about library book content
mentions.author: Sarah Asch
mentions.content_excerpt: Districts using ChatGPT for SB 13 compliance — Pearland ISD reviewed 1,400 books, ChatGPT incorrectly flagged content. Bookmarked NOT named despite covering exact problem Bookmarked solves.

mentions.headline: Comal ISD trustees approve first book list following SB13
mentions.content_excerpt: Local paper on Comal ISD trustees approving first SB 13-compliant acquisition list.

mentions.headline: Leander ISD faces backlash after pulling books under SB12 review
mentions.content_excerpt: Local community paper coverage of parent/student pushback on Leander book pulls.

mentions.headline: Leander ISD pauses dozens of books in classrooms in response to state law
mentions.content_excerpt: Local FOX affiliate. LISD pauses dozens of classroom books under SB 12 citing 'abundance of caution.'

mentions.headline: Overcomplying With New Laws, Texas School District Closes Entire Library, Others Remove Dozens of Books
mentions.content_excerpt: PEN America press release on Texas districts overcomplying with SB 13. Leads with New Braunfels ISD indefinite closure of all non-elementary libraries. Documents Leander ISD removal of 40 books including To Kill a Mockingbird, House on Mango Street using AI for DEI content review.

mentions.headline: Texas School Administrators Use A.I. To Ban Books Like TO KILL A MOCKINGBIRD
mentions.content_excerpt: Book Riot coverage of Leander ISD AI-driven book removals under SB 12 (and SB 13 context). Names removed/temporarily-removed titles: To Kill a Mockingbird, Les Misérables, House on Mango Street, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass, The Devil's Arithmetic. National-critic framing.

mentions.headline: McKinney ISD Changes Its Definition of Obscenity
mentions.author: Frank Strong
mentions.content_excerpt: Analysis of McKinney ISD changing its obscenity definition to enable broader removals.

mentions.headline: Bookmarked: Killeen ISD's Tool For Supporting Student Reading Habits
mentions.author: Tasha Stevens
mentions.content_excerpt: Positive local radio coverage. Parent engagement tools in Killeen ISD. No censorship framing.

mentions.headline: Comal ISD updates library materials policy following passage of Senate Bill 13
mentions.content_excerpt: Community Impact coverage of Comal ISD adopting acquisition/review policy under SB 13. Board must approve new materials; public comment process.

mentions.headline: UPDATED: Every Texas School District Book Ban
mentions.content_excerpt: Running tally of TX districts with book bans. Texas ranks #2 nationally behind Florida in 2024-25 per PEN America.

mentions.headline: This beloved children's book was just banned in Texas—along with hundreds more
mentions.content_excerpt: National pickup of the Pinkalicious-in-Lamar-CISD story.

mentions.headline: Lamar CISD bans more than 700 books from its libraries before start of 2025-26 school year
mentions.content_excerpt: Widely-syndicated local piece on LCISD 700-book ban. Includes Pinkalicious (children's book) going national for controversy.

mentions.headline: Pinkalicious and 700+ other titles removed from Lamar Consolidated ISD, near Houston
mentions.content_excerpt: Records-based disclosure: 700+ books removed from LCISD classrooms and libraries at start of 2025-26 year. Titles include Pinkalicious, Paperbag Princess, The Bluest Eye, Maus 1/2, Drama (Telgemeier), Black Boy Joy, House on Mango Street. District cited 'gender fluidity' and HB 900 as reasons.

mentions.headline: Killeen ISD parents can track student reading choices
mentions.content_excerpt: Local TV coverage. Positive parent access framing.

mentions.headline: KISD expands library oversight for parents
mentions.content_excerpt: Local newspaper. Straightforward district tech adoption reporting.

mentions.headline: Conroe ISD Trustees Vote To Throw Out Thousands Of Classroom Books
mentions.content_excerpt: Conroe ISD board voted to dispose of 2,800+ books. 589 titles with multiple copies. Top to be disposed: Brave New World (368 copies), Lord of the Flies (329), The Hate U Give (172), The Bluest Eye (61), The Color Purple (47). 149 of the 589 titles were formally banned.

mentions.headline: Bill to restrict school library books approved by lawmakers
mentions.author: Aaron Torres
mentions.content_excerpt: Coverage of final legislative passage of SB 13 (House 81-48, Senate 23-8). Gives school boards final say over what materials are allowed in school libraries. Bills creates framework for board removal based on complaints. Cited: Lamar CISD removed ~300 works ahead of 2025-26 school year per Texas Freedom to Read Project. PEN America: Texas second only to Florida in book bans.

mentions.headline: Katy ISD students call for repeal of book and gender policies following school board election
mentions.content_excerpt: Public radio coverage following Katy ISD school board shakeup. Students call for policy repeal.

mentions.headline: Katy ISD community calls for transgender, banned book policy changes under new leadership
mentions.content_excerpt: Houston FOX affiliate on Katy ISD board president oust and policy pushback.

mentions.headline: A Texas school leader says material about diversity in state-approved textbooks violated the law
mentions.content_excerpt: CFISD board member Natalie Blasingame proposed stripping 13+ chapters from 5 state-approved textbooks. 6-1 vote removed chapters on climate change, vaccines, cultural diversity, depopulation, human environmental impact. Biology, environmental science, health science, principles of education.

mentions.headline: In Conroe ISD the Books Are Quietly Disappearing Off Classroom Shelves
mentions.content_excerpt: Investigation of Conroe ISD classroom book removals via informal review rather than formal challenge process. Teachers asked to box up and return thousands of books for 'disposal.' Grows a pattern of stealth removals.

mentions.headline: The People Behind BookmarkED Are Behind Book Bans in Texas
mentions.author: Kelly Jensen
mentions.content_excerpt: Investigates founders' ties to book-challenge movements. Names Jason Cochrane (Krum ISD superintendent) as co-owner. Cites Moak Casey lobbying firm equity stake.

mentions.headline: How The BookmarkED/OnShelf App, Created to Help Schools Ban Books, Fuels Them Instead
mentions.author: Kelly Jensen
mentions.content_excerpt: Argues OnShelf was designed to facilitate book removal. Product-as-weapon framing.

mentions.headline: BookmarkED App Connected to Book Ban Efforts in Texas
mentions.author: Kelly Jensen
mentions.content_excerpt: Connects BookmarkED to organized book-ban efforts. Names Jason Cochrane connection.

mentions.headline: OnShelf by Bookmarked
mentions.content_excerpt: Advocacy blog review of OnShelf product.

mentions.headline: Lamar CISD to remove, restrict books about gender fluidity from school libraries and instructional material
mentions.content_excerpt: Houston ABC affiliate. 5-1 board vote to remove/restrict gender-fluidity books. Follows other Houston-area districts.

mentions.headline: 'Unintended consequences': Conroe ISD board to alter book review policy after 25 A.P. books banned
mentions.content_excerpt: Local TV coverage. 25 AP books swept up in Conroe policy, triggering policy revision.

mentions.headline: Cy-Fair ISD trustees gain power in book banning process
mentions.content_excerpt: ABC13 on CFISD policy changes expanding trustee authority over book challenges.

mentions.headline: ACLU demands San Antonio's North East ISD end ban on 110 books it removed from its libraries
mentions.content_excerpt: Alt-weekly coverage. ACLU demands NEISD end 110-book ban.

mentions.headline: Manufacturing Problems with School and Library Books to Cash in on Solutions
mentions.author: Kelly Jensen
mentions.content_excerpt: Weekly censorship roundup grouping Bookmarked with organizations profiting from book ban movement.

mentions.headline: Conroe ISD school board strengthens controversial library review process, OK's removing book from some schools
mentions.content_excerpt: Public radio coverage of Conroe board strengthening review policy, including only-one-trustee-appeal provision.

mentions.headline: BookmarkED Addresses the State of Challenged Books with Industry-First Technology
mentions.content_excerpt: Ed-tech trade press pickup of BusinessWire release. Neutral coverage.

mentions.headline: BookmarkED Addresses the State of Challenged Books with Industry-First Technology
mentions.content_excerpt: Official launch press release. BookmarkED as industry-first technology for parental choice in school libraries.

mentions.headline: Why This School District Used A.I. to Help Determine Which Books to Ban
mentions.author: Sarah Kuta
mentions.content_excerpt: Iowa's Mason City used ChatGPT — removed 19 titles. Canonical 'districts using AI for book review' story. Evergreen contrast point: general AI vs. purpose-built tools.

mentions.headline: PEN America: Using Artificial Intelligence to Ban Books Only Makes the Problem Worse
mentions.content_excerpt: Press release about Iowa Mason City School District using ChatGPT to decide which books to remove. Does NOT mention Bookmarked directly. Relevant as industry context — establishes PEN America's position that AI in book removal is 'deeply misguided.' Liz Woolery quoted. Published August 2023, predates Bookmarked's current market presence.
mentions.relevance_note: Industry context only — PEN America opposes AI in book curation generally. This position frames their criticism of Bookmarked in later articles.

mentions.headline: The First App to 'Help' Libraries and Schools with Book Bans Has Arrived — It's Not What It Seems
mentions.author: Kelly Jensen
mentions.content_excerpt: Earliest Book Riot coverage. Skeptical framing of BookmarkED's arrival as 'not what it seems.'

mentions.headline: BookmarkED Launches New Tech Solution to Aid Texas Schools
mentions.content_excerpt: Launch coverage. Neutral business announcement.

mentions.headline: Granbury ISD Being Investigated for Removing LGBTQ Books From School Libraries
mentions.content_excerpt: DFW NBC affiliate on federal investigation of Granbury ISD superintendent's order to remove LGBTQ books.

mentions.headline: Carroll ISD adopts new book policy for classrooms, libraries
mentions.content_excerpt: Community Impact DFW coverage of Carroll ISD (Southlake) adopting new EFA/EFB book policy.

mentions.headline: Granbury, Texas, School District Removes 130 Books While Rewriting Book Review Process
mentions.content_excerpt: Advocacy coverage of Granbury's 130-book removal and policy rewrite.

mentions.headline: NEISD's removal of library books may be unconstitutional, expert says
mentions.content_excerpt: Local SA nonprofit news. Legal experts questioning constitutionality of NEISD 400+ book pull.

mentions.headline: Texas School District Pulls 400+ Books From Shelves
mentions.content_excerpt: Book Riot on NEISD pulling 400+ books after a state lawmaker's inquiry. Titles included art history, Greek/Roman mythology, LGBTQ health resources.

mentions.headline: Texas school district pulls 400 books from library shelves for review after legislator's inquiry
mentions.content_excerpt: National NBC coverage of NEISD 400+ book review triggered by a state lawmaker.
